name: Letna Park
description: A massive park sitting high above the Vltava River, offering arguably the best panoramic views. Features a massive metronome and a very popular beer garden.

name: Prague Castle
description: The largest ancient castle complex in the world. A sprawling mix of palaces, courtyards, and history dominating the city skyline.

name: St. Vitus Cathedral
description: An incredibly striking Gothic masterpiece. Famous for its towering spires and stunning stained glass.

name: Golden Lane
description: A historic street of tiny, colorful houses built into the castle walls in the 16th century. Franz Kafka briefly lived in house No. 22.

name: Strahov Monastery Library
description: One of the most beautiful libraries in the world, featuring stunning Baroque ceiling frescoes and ancient globes. Major dark academia vibes.

name: Petrin Lookout Tower
description: A mini-Eiffel Tower located at the top of Petrin Hill. Climbing the 299 steps rewards you with the highest vantage point in Prague.

name: Mirror Maze
description: A small, retro amusement attraction on Petrin Hill housed in a mini-castle. A fun, quick, and slightly trippy optical illusion.

name: Memorial to the Victims of Communism
description: A striking and haunting series of bronze statues descending a staircase, visually decaying to represent the toll of the communist era.

name: National Theatre
description: The stunning neo-Renaissance alma mater of Czech opera, acting, and ballet. A symbol of Czech national identity.

name: Franz Kafka Rotating Head
description: A massive, mesmerizing kinetic sculpture of writer Franz Kafka's head made of 42 rotating mirrored panels by artist David Černý.

name: Wenceslas Square
description: More of a broad boulevard than a square, this is the commercial heart of Prague and the historic site of many Czech uprisings.

name: Klementinum Library
description: An astoundingly beautiful Baroque library and astronomical tower. It looks like something straight out of Hogwarts.

name: Charles Bridge
description: The legendary 14th-century stone bridge crossing the Vltava, lined with 30 towering, soot-darkened statues of saints. Best visited at dawn.

name: Lennon Wall
description: A continually changing wall covered in John Lennon-inspired graffiti and lyrics from Beatles songs. A historic symbol of peace and free speech.

name: Prague's Narrowest Street
description: A pedestrian passageway so incredibly narrow (barely 50cm wide) that it has its own pedestrian traffic light to prevent people from getting stuck.

name: Wallenstein Garden
description: A stunning, pristine Baroque garden featuring roaming white peacocks and a massive, deeply bizarre artificial dripstone wall that looks like skulls.

name: Vysehrad Fortress
description: A historic fort set high on a cliff above the river. Contains a beautiful basilica, sweeping views, and an incredibly gothic national cemetery.

name: Dancing House
description: Frank Gehry's surreal, modern architectural masterpiece that sharply contrasts with Prague's historic buildings. It looks like two dancers (Fred and Ginger).

name: National Museum
description: A monumental, recently renovated museum at the top of Wenceslas Square dominating the skyline. Incredible interior architecture and natural history exhibits.

name: Riegrovy Sady
description: A sprawling park popular with locals, famous for its massive beer garden and sloping hills that offer one of the best sunset views of the castle.

name: Powder Tower
description: A stark, blackened Gothic tower that serves as one of the original 13 city gates. It was once used to store gunpowder in the 17th century.

name: Church of Our Lady before Tyn
description: The quintessential fairytale Gothic church with imposing, black multi-pronged spires that dominate the Old Town Square. Super moody and striking.

name: Spanish Synagogue
description: Known as the most beautiful synagogue in Europe, featuring absolutely breathtaking, intricate Moorish interior design and gold leaf detailing.

name: Astronomical Clock
description: The oldest operating astronomical clock in the world. Crowds gather every hour to watch the mechanical 'Walk of the Apostles' and the skeletal figure of Death strike the bell.

name: Hanging Freud
description: If you look up while walking in Old Town, you'll see a life-sized sculpture of Sigmund Freud dangling by one hand from a roof. A classic David Černý prank.

name: Cihelna Park Giant Rats (Crawling Babies)
description: Located in Kampa Park near the river, these massive, terrifying, faceless bronze babies (by David Černý) crawl around the lawn. Deeply weird and memorable.

name: St. Nicholas Church
description: Considered the greatest example of Prague Baroque architecture. The interior is a massive explosion of marble, gold, and a frescoed dome. Mozart once played the organ here.

name: Water Mill with Goblin Statue (Kabourek)
description: A picturesque old water wheel on the Certovka canal, guarded by a quaint statue of a traditional Czech water goblin smoking a pipe.

name: The Playground
description: Formerly Hamleys, this is a massive multi-level toy store and indoor amusement park right in the center of Prague. Great for a quick burst of chaotic energy.

name: Sex Machines Museum
description: The only museum in the world strictly dedicated to sex machines. An incredibly bizarre, slightly creepy, and uniquely Prague oddity.

name: Prague Beer Museum
description: Part museum, part massive pub. Learn about the legendary history of Czech brewing and finish by pulling your own pints and doing a massive tasting flight.

name: Cafe Savoy
description: A stunning, classic First Republic era cafe with an ornate Neo-Renaissance ceiling. Perfect for an upscale coffee and traditional Czech pastries.

name: CukrCafe Blackulus
description: A cozy, highly-rated local sweet shop and cafe, perfect for a strong coffee and an oversized slice of homemade cake.

name: (A)VOID Cafe
description: Built directly into the stone vaults on the riverbank (Naplavka). A floating gallery, cafe, and bar with a gritty, hyper-indie, and bohemian vibe.

name: GoldenEgg
description: A fast-casual, modern breakfast joint specializing in heavily stacked, gourmet egg sandwiches. Excellent hangover cure.

name: Prague Castle Starbucks
description: Normally a chain wouldn't make the list, but this specific location is built right on the castle ramparts and offers one of the most stunning panoramic terrace views in the city.

name: Havels Market
description: One of the oldest markets in Prague (dating back to 1232). A mix of fresh produce, souvenirs, and quick, cheap Czech street food bites.

name: Medieval Tavern U krale brabantskeho
description: One of the oldest pubs in Prague. Lit only by candles, featuring skulls, fire dancers, heavy tankards of beer, and an aggressively authentic medieval atmosphere.

name: U Fleku
description: A massive, historic brewery and restaurant that has been brewing its own dark lager continuously for over 500 years. Expect accordions and communal tables.

name: Knedlin
description: A hyper-modern, colorful spot solely dedicated to traditional Czech dumplings (knedliky), but stuffed with wild, sweet, and savory modern fillings.

name: Havelska Koruna
description: An authentic, no-frills, cafeteria-style Czech canteen. Grab a tray and point at massive plates of cheap, delicious traditional goulash and dumplings.

name: U Kalendu
description: A historic riverside pub that was recently reborn as an incredibly trendy craft bakery by day and an elevated modern Czech restaurant by night.

name: Vytopna Railway Restaurant
description: A restaurant right on Wenceslas Square where 900 meters of miniature railway tracks run across the tables, and model trains deliver beer directly to your seat.

name: Anonymus Shrink's Office
description: A bizarre, hidden speakeasy themed around Rorschach tests and psychiatry. The cocktail menu is hidden, and drinks are prescribed to you based on psychological tests.

name: Nightmare Horror Bar
description: A dark, basement bar dedicated entirely to classic horror movies. Features creepy props, heavy metal vibes, and themed cocktails in a genuinely spooky atmosphere.

name: Green Flamingo Absinthe Bar
description: A moody, intimate spot dedicated to the Green Fairy. They serve real, high-quality absinthe prepared the traditional way with a water drip.

name: My People Bar
description: A highly-rated, cozy craft cocktail bar hidden in the center. Known for incredible mixologists and a very welcoming, friendly atmosphere.

name: Robotic Bar Prague
description: A deeply futuristic novelty bar where massive robotic arms mix, shake, and pour your cocktails completely autonomously.

name: Kellyxir Alchemical Pub
description: A magical pub themed around Prague's historic alchemists. Drinks are served in bubbling test tubes, beakers, and dry-ice cauldrons.

name: Fat Cat Prague
description: A lively, loud craft beer house and burger joint located right in the center. Great for huge pints of IPA and watching sports.

name: Hany Bany
description: A legendary, slightly gritty student bar with cheap beer, retro punk decor, and a distinctly alternative, underground vibe.

name: Duplex
description: A massive, glass-walled rooftop club sitting right on top of Wenceslas Square. High-end, flashy, and features top international DJs.

name: Cafe 80s
description: A pure nostalgia trip. A neon-soaked club dedicated entirely to 80s pop hits, light-up dance floors, and retro arcade games.

name: Karlovy Lazne
description: The biggest music club in Central Europe. Spans 5 entire floors, each playing a completely different genre of music.
